---
title: "Supplementary: Feasibility evaluation and a work estimate toward a whole-cell platelet model"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
    include-in-header: ../../pandoc-header.tex
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
bibliography: ../references.bib
csl: ../harvard-leeds-beckett-university.csl
---

```{=html}
<!--
SKELETON. Planning notes in *[italic brackets]*. This is the most
intellectually valuable supplement and the spine of the PhD proposal. IMPORTANT:
the feasibility VERDICT itself belongs in the main thesis Discussion (don't bury
the punchline in an appendix); this supplement carries the DETAILED breakdown.
Keep the claim MODEST throughout (decision 4): the approach is worth pushing
further — not "a WCM is feasible / done". Reasoning developed in
`reports/thesis/review-part-2.md` §4.
-->
```

## Purpose

*[State the relationship to the main thesis: the Discussion gives the verdict; this gives the module-by-module evidence and the work estimate behind it.]*

## What would count as a "genuine" whole-cell platelet model

*[Define the target. A mechanistic, mass-balanced, copy-number-accounted model spanning the major functional modules of the platelet, validated per module. Distinguish from (a) a single-pathway model and (b) a data-driven "virtual cell" (cross-ref the AI supplement).]*

## How strongly is feasibility proved? — a three-tier assessment

*[The honest core. Reproduce and expand the tiered framing:]*

- **Weak claim — proved.** *[One Ca²⁺ pathway modelled mechanistically and validated (Dolan 5/5, resting state held, no hand-fitted forcing).]*
- **Medium claim — partially shown.** *[The architecture ports to platelet biology (adaptation ✓) and is extensible (asserted, NOT yet demonstrated — only one pathway exists; no second process sharing state). Name this as the honest soft spot.]*
- **Strong claim — not proved, not claimed.** *[A complete WCM is feasible. Defensible substitute: viable proof-of-concept; principal risk is data, not architecture/computation [@szigeti2017].]*

## Module-by-module breakdown

*[Table: module | status | primary data source(s) | data availability | rough effort. Rows: Ca²⁺ signalling core (DONE); metabolism / mitochondrial ATP + glycolysis; cytoskeleton / shape change; granule biogenesis + secretion; integrin αIIbβ3 inside-out/outside-in; full GPCR receptor complement; membrane lipid / PI dynamics; procoagulant (PS exposure). Flag the 2-3 modules likely blocked on missing wet-lab data.]*

## The anucleate tractability argument

*[The platelet's key advantage: no transcription, translation, replication, or division — exactly where Karr 2012 [@karr2012] spent the most effort. This is the strongest single feasibility argument; make it explicitly.]*

## The binding constraint: data, not computation

*[Argue, with evidence, that calibration/data curation dominates cost, not coding or solver time. The community blueprint [@szigeti2017] and the PI-cycle study [@mazet2020] reach the same conclusion; the k₃ episode is first-hand evidence. AI-assisted coding compresses engineering, not data cost.]*

## A rough work estimate

*[Reasoned, order-of-magnitude, with caveats. ~8-15 major modules; small team (2-4 people); ~3-5 years; dominated by data, with 2-3 modules potentially infeasible today without new measurement. Present as person-years and a dependency on data availability, NOT a coding schedule. Show the reasoning so it reads as judgement, not a guess.]*

## Risks and unknowns

*[What could make this harder than estimated: data that doesn't exist; modules that don't compose cleanly; validation experiments that aren't available; the well-mixed simplification breaking down (microdomains). Honest about each.]*

## Verdict

*[Modest close: the proof-of-concept is encouraging and the obstacles are well-characterised enough to justify pushing the idea further. Mirror, don't duplicate, the main-thesis Discussion.]*

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
